studyTexts.title: Work buy consume die
studyTexts.content:
1- If i won the 100,000 crowns in the lottery, I will use it to establish a small business or invest it in Stockholm Nasdaq. I think at 100,000 crowns is a quite small amount to start spend it so in my opinion investing is good in the beginning especially  at investing can gain 7-10% profit every year.

2- If i won 10 million Swedish crowns i will divide it to 2 parts one of them to open my own small business while the remain will used for down payment of cottage in Gothenburg countryside 

3- If I'm lucky and won 10 million Swedish crowns that means at my dream has achieved . I will buy my own cottage and pay for it in full and open my small business also i will spend the remain amount in investing. Thereby , i will have my own home, a business that insurance my income and that mean i will achieved 
the financial freedom.

studyTexts.title: Will consumerism be the death of mankind?
studyTexts.content:
Subject / Question
Will consumerism be the death of mankind?

Outline
1-What consumerism is
2-Benefits of consumerism
3-Environmental consequences
4-Social and psychological consequences
5-Possible solutions
6-Conclusion

Introduction
Modern society offers consumers easier access to goods than ever before. Fashion trends, advertising, and online shopping encourage people to purchase more products. As a result, consumerism has become an important part of daily life. Many people buy items not only because they need them, but also because they want them.

-Benefits of consumerism
Consumerism is the tendency to buy. This tendency is encouraged by advertising, the fashion industry, and modern marketing strategies. These influences often make people feel that they need products even when they do not. in addition the revolution of e-commerce encourage made it easier to purchase from home. All of this led to a growing concerns whether consumerism poses a threat to humanity?

Main Body
consumerism can provide several advantages to consumers in many ways. First, High competition between companies can reduce the prices to attract the customers. For example, supermarkets such as Lidl and ICA often offer discounts and special deals.  Furthermore the consumerism advertising show the consumer a different alternative to buy the product . that make it easy to compare prices before making a purchase. In addition people can easily check whether  a product is available and where it can be found. 
At a result the consumerism can provide convenience and and more choices to the people.

Environmental problem . The more purchase mean more waste for example when the traders offer 2 pieces for 1 or discount if the customer purchase more this lead the customer to follow the offer and waste more money. This  lead also to buy more product therefore the pollution, resource depletion is increased also. in addition that need to increase the factories, delivery, packing and energy consumption. As a result carbon emission increase. For example if millions people buy new phone every year that will trigger many process like made it in factories, packing, transport it to the customer., all of which contribute to environmental pollution.

Social and Psychological  Consequences is represent in some people spend money more than they have such example using Klarna or whatever of convenience methods and that my lead to debt.
Secondly people compare themselves with others thus create dissatisfaction. Consumerism can have negative social effects.

Possible Solution can be buying from second hand stores, recycling and do not attract to the tempt announces.

In conclusion, consumerism offers several benefits, such as lower prices, greater convenience, and more choices for consumers. However, it also contributes to environmental problems, resource depletion, and social challenges. Therefore, consumerism should be managed responsibly through sustainable consumption and greater consumer awareness. Although it is a serious hazard, it is unlikely to lead to the death of mankind

studyTexts.title: Why the British Empire expanded and how it changed the world.
studyTexts.content:
For centuries, empires have changed the map of the world by conquering territories, controlling trade, and spreading their culture.

The British Empire expanded because of economic interests, military power, and political ambition, and its expansion changed the world by exploiting many lands, spreading British influence, and creating both suffering and development in different regions.

The British Empire expanded mainly because of economic interests, military strength, and political ambition.

Britain wanted more wealth, more power, and more control over global trade. Its navy and strong economy helped it reach and control many regions.

One of the strongest motives was the search for raw materials and new markets.

For example, Britain controlled parts of India and used its resources, trade routes, and labor to strengthen the empire.

This shows that expansion was not only about ruling land, but also about gaining economic advantage.

In sum, the British Empire expanded because economic profit, military strength, and political ambition worked together to make colonisation possible.

its expansion changed the world by exploiting many lands, spreading British influence, and creating both suffering and development

British rule affected politics, economies, languages, education systems, and cultures in many colonies. Some changes created new institutions and connections, but many people also lost control over their land, labor, and resources.

One major effect was the spread of the English language and British-style education and government.

For example, English became an important language in countries such as India, Canada, Australia, and many parts of Africa.

This shows that British rule left a long-lasting cultural and political influence even after the empire ended.

However, the empire also caused serious harm by taking resources and limiting the freedom of local people.

In many colonies, local economies were shaped to serve British trade rather than the needs of the local population.

As a result, the empire created inequality and suffering that continued to affect some societies after independence.

In sum, the British Empire changed the world in both positive and negative ways, leaving behind language, institutions, and global connections, but also exploitation, inequality, and painful historical memories.

In conclusion, the British Empire expanded because Britain wanted wealth, power, and control over global trade. Its expansion changed the world by spreading the English language, British institutions, and international connections, but it also caused exploitation and suffering in many colonies. In my opinion, it is important to study the British Empire honestly, because its history shows how power can create both progress and deep injustice.

studyTexts.title: Advantages and disadvantages
studyTexts.content:
Renting or buying, Among of these two options many factors and features that can allow or pretend the people of buying or renting a house.
However some people like to get a house other people dislike that and prefer to rent an apartment. Here emerge a significant important wonder.
What is the advantages and disadvantages of renting a house?

In this essay i will discuss why renting can allow for more flexible lifestyle, but why it can totally be a harmful for a wallet?

The main benefit of renting a house , rather than buying one, is because you can move anytime. .

That can be happened in case your job location change to other office or other project.

The main benefit of renting a house instead of buying one is because you can move at any time.

For example if your manager pick you to do your work in a far spot , There you want to ride you car more than two hours or ride a train. Thus, to move to be close to the new position is the first solution you will think about.

studyTexts.title: Empire British
studyTexts.content:
In the late 19th century, the empire British colonized approximately a quarter of world wide's area and more the quarter of population.
To understand this situation we need to analyze the reasons which push British to conquer other lands and what the consequences of that colonization.

This essay will explain at the empire British expanded due to economical interests and the related effect to that expand to change the social and economical system in it's colonies.

One main reason due empire British to conquer other lands is the economic expansion and needed to raw materials which was a result of industrial revolution in British. It's important to consider at the growth of  trading and  industry was the strongest reason to allow empire British to colonize other lands.

According to Britannica, “expanding world markets and sources of inexpensive raw materials and food” (Magdoff & The Editors of Encyclopaedia Britannica, 2026, p. 7).

The colonies supplied empire with raw material like sugar and tobacco, in other hand British provide them by the manufactured goods candy and cigarettes. “such as tobacco and sugar” (The Editors of Encyclopaedia Britannica, 2026, p. 4).

Therefore, The needed of raw materials and new markets drove British to expand to achieve it's interests.

It is important to consider that the expand lead to many significant  consequences and effects, one of them is impact the social and economical system of the colonies in order to serve the empire targets and intentions.

A clear example of this is  mentioned in the following quotation, “As British sovereignty spread, new land-revenue devices were soon instituted” (Magdoff & The Editors of Encyclopaedia Britannica, 2026, p. 3).

Empire British imposed new land-revenue system and that cause direct  bad influence and weekend the village system as well as the depend on their own effort to get food from agricultural crop. So this is a social effect also that emerge from the land-revenue.

Clearly, Expansion and domination of empire British led to an enormous impact to reform the economical and social   system and weekend the population especially in the villages.

In conclusion, The empire British colonize and expand the other countries in order to achieve it's interests and benefits, While the colonies population got suffer, economical difficulties and experienced asocial changes . In my opinion, it is danger to put the interests before the humanity and stability of the society.

Sources/Criticism

1- The Editors of Encyclopaedia Britannica. (2026, May 28). British Empire. Encyclopaedia Britannica. https://www.britannica.com/place/British-Empire
Criticism: This source reliable and trustable because it's published by Encyclopaedia Britannica and written by Britannica Editorss  
2-Magdoff, H., & The Editors of Encyclopaedia Britannica. (2026, May 22). Loss of the American colonies. Encyclopaedia Britannica. https://www.britannica.com/topic/Western-colonialism/Loss-of-the-American-colonies
Criticism: This source is valuable, provide many details . I's wrote by Harry Magdoff but the publication by Britannica increase the source reliability.

Criticism: This source is valuable, provide many details . I's wrote by Harry Magdoff but the publication by Britannica increase the source reliability.

Cause: Economic expansion driven by trade and the Industrial Revolution.

Effect: Economic and social systems in colonized regions were transformed.
